data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Chip Stocks Slide, Biren Technology Drops Over 18%
data.description:
Hong Kong-listed chip stocks continued to fall in afternoon trading, led by sharp declines in several names. According to Jin10, Biren Technology (06082.HK) fell more than 18%, while GigaDevice Semiconductor (03986.HK) dropped more than 15%.

SMIC (00981.HK), Innoscience (02577.HK), and Solomon Systech (02878.HK) also moved lower.

data.title: Asian Stocks Mostly Decline as Chip Shares Sell Off
data.description:
According to Associated Press, Asian shares mostly declined Thursday on heavy selling of computer chip stocks, while US futures were little changed after modest losses on Wall Street. South Korea's benchmark Kospi index sank 5.1% to 7,877.45, with memory chip maker SK Hynix losing 7.7% and Samsung Electronics tumbling 6.4%. 
Tokyo's Nikkei 225 lost 1.5% to 69,443.16 as chip equipment maker Tokyo Electron shed 5.6%. Taiwan's Taiex declined 1.1% with TSMC falling 1.8%. Hong Kong's Hang Seng rose 0.8% to 23,060.63, with BYD shares jumping 8.7% after reporting a second straight month of rising sales. The Shanghai Composite fell 0.9% to 4,075.58. Australia's S&P/ASX 200 edged 0.1% lower to 8,710.30, while India's Sensex climbed 0.5%. 
Oil prices fell after US and Iranian negotiators met separately with mediators from Qatar and Pakistan on Wednesday as traders eyed developments toward a permanent end to the war in Iran. 
The Kospi and Nikkei 225 have gained about 85% and 34% respectively so far this year, driven by surging demand for artificial intelligence.

data.title: Report: FBI Director Kash Patel missed required disclosure of six-figure MSTR buy
data.description: FBI Director Kash Patel failed to timely disclose a six-figure purchase of Strategy (MSTR) stock, according to CoinDesk, citing a report by nonpartisan outlet NOTUS. Patel reportedly bought $100,001 to $250,000 of MSTR on Nov. 21 but did not report the trade until May 26, telling the Office of Government Ethics he “inadvertently omitted” it due to a “miscommunication.” Under the STOCK Act, executive branch officials must disclose stock trades over $1,000 within 45 days.

data.title: BitGo’s Ang: Volatility Bolsters Case for Digital-Asset Infrastructure
data.description:
Angela Ang, Asia-Pacific managing director at BitGo, said shifts from retail speculation to institutional investors are driving demand for secure custody and compliant digital-asset infrastructure, according to Bloomberg.

Her comments followed bitcoin falling to a fresh 21-month low. Ang spoke with Haslinda Amin on “Insight with Haslinda Amin.”

data.title: WORLD CUP | Mexico fans’ goal celebrations vs Ecuador triggered “significant” tremors
data.description: Mass celebrations by Mexico fans after two goals in a World Cup knockout match against Ecuador produced “significant” local vibrations recorded on seismographs, Mexico’s Digital Platform for Early Warning and Comprehensive Risk Management (SASSLA) said. SASSLA said it detected tremors after Julián Quiñones’ goal and again after Raúl Jiménez scored at the 31st minute in Mexico’s 2-0 win, which qualified Mexico for the last 16; three people were also crushed to death in Mexico City after the match as crowds celebrated, according to Yahoo Sports.

data.title: China Expands Occupational Injury Insurance Pilot for New-Form Employment Workers Nationwide
data.description:
China has expanded a pilot occupational injury insurance program for workers in new forms of employment nationwide starting this month.

According to Jin10, the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Security said the rollout will fully extend the pilot across the country.

Among newly included platform companies, nine are in the mobility sector: T3 Mobility, Huaxiaozhu Mobility, Sunshine Mobility, Ruqi Mobility, Xiangdao Mobility, Jishi Mobility, Fengyun Mobility, Shouqi Ride-Hailing, and Meituan Ride-Hailing.

Five newly included platforms are in the on-demand delivery sector: Xiaoxiang Supermarket, Hema Fresh, Dingdong Maicai, Pupu Supermarket, and UU Paotui.

On this basis, provincial authorities will follow a “ready first, include first” approach to bring small and medium-sized platform companies registered in their provinces into the pilot across three pilot industries within the year.

data.title: Congo Plans Dual-Currency Stock Exchange to Attract Investors
data.description:
The Democratic Republic of Congo is drawing up plans for its first stock exchange, including a dual-currency market, as it seeks to attract more investment, according to Bloomberg.

The move comes as the economy benefits from soaring demand for minerals critical to the AI buildout.

data.title: Nomura Economist Says Japan PM’s Economic Blueprint May Affect BOJ Rate-Hike Timing
data.description:
A Nomura Research Institute economist said Japan’s prime minister could use an economic policy blueprint to influence the timing of the Bank of Japan’s next interest-rate move.

According to Jin10, economist Takahide Kiuchi said that if Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi uses her “Basic Policy” economic blueprint—expected to receive cabinet approval this month—to block further rate hikes, it could delay the BOJ’s next step.

Kiuchi said that even if the government opposes higher rates, the BOJ would still raise interest rates when it deems it necessary, but it may to some extent respect the government’s preferences on timing.

He added that government pressure against BOJ rate hikes could further weaken the yen and push bond prices lower, which he said would undermine the stability of Japan’s economy and financial markets.

data.title: Palm Oil Slips on Weaker Crude, Malaysia Output Outlook
data.description: Palm oil edged lower, pressured by declining crude prices and expectations for strong production from Malaysia, the world’s second-biggest grower, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Yen Edges Higher With Traders on Alert for Risk of Intervention
data.description: The yen strengthened slightly against the dollar Thursday amid speculation that Japan may intervene again to support the currency without warning from officials, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Michael Burry Expands AI-Related Short Positions, Including Tesla and Chip ETF
data.description:
Michael Burry has expanded his AI-related short positions, with newly disclosed targets including Tesla, Caterpillar, Applied Materials, and an ETF that tracks chip manufacturers.

According to Odaily, Burry has maintained a bearish view on AI-linked stocks for months, saying that once the AI boom cools, related asset prices could face a correction.

In a recent Substack post, Burry wrote that South Korea’s announcement of a large-scale chip investment plan helped push chip stocks higher. He described the move as “the beginning of the end” and questioned whether continued capital spending tied to AI industry expansion can ultimately translate into corresponding returns.

data.title: Guolian Minsheng Securities, Edmond de Rothschild Bank to Sell 58.409% Stake in China Ocean Fund for RMB267 Million
data.description:
Guolian Minsheng Securities (601456.SH) said it has signed an equity transaction contract with Chengdu Jiaozixinxing Financial Investment Group to sell its 33.409% stake in China Ocean Fund Management, alongside a 25% stake held by France’s Edmond de Rothschild Bank, according to Jiemian News. The contract was signed on June 30 after the buyer emerged as an intended transferee during a public listing process.

The total consideration is RMB267 million, including RMB153 million for Guolian Minsheng Securities’ stake. The company said it remains uncertain whether Jiaozixinxing can obtain the required qualification to become a shareholder of a public fund manager, as the deal still needs approval from the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC). Jiaozixinxing has paid a RMB53.4 million deposit to the Shanghai United Assets and Equity Exchange.

China Ocean Fund was jointly listed for transfer on April 3, with floor prices of RMB153 million and RMB114 million for the two stakes, and the listing period ended on June 3. As of end-Q1, the fund manager had RMB8.251 billion in public fund AUM, Wind data showed.

data.title: RBNZ Won’t Add Member to Policy Committee Ahead of Election
data.description: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand will not add a member to its Monetary Policy Committee ahead of the November election, leaving the panel with three internal and three external members, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Polymarket Odds of the Clarity Act Becoming Law in 2026 Fall to 39% Amid Senate Debate
data.description:
Sen. Cynthia Lummis defended the Clarity Act, saying it includes more than 16 safeguards aimed at addressing illicit finance risks.

According to NS3.AI, Sen. Elizabeth Warren argued that the digital-asset bill would weaken existing standards.

Market expectations on the bill’s prospects also shifted, with Polymarket placing the odds of the Clarity Act becoming law in 2026 at 39%, down from earlier levels.

data.title: STOCKS | Carry Wealth Holdings Says Court Order Seeks to Restrict Transfer of 411 Million Shares
data.description:
Carry Wealth Holdings (0643) said it received an originating summons stop notice issued by the High Court on June 30 this year, according to Ming Pao.

The company said the notice was filed by applicant Giant Fortune International Development and seeks to restrict the transfer of 411 million shares currently registered under MARS Worldwide, as well as the payment of any dividends or interest on those shares, until 14 days’ notice has been given to Giant Fortune’s legal representative.

data.title: STOCKS | Leapmotor Chairman Zhu Jiangming and Shareholder Fu Liquan Buy 4.81M H Shares
data.description:
Leapmotor (9863) said shareholder, chairman and CEO Zhu Jiangming and shareholder Fu Liquan bought a combined 4.81 million H shares in the market at an average price of about HK$35.86 per share, according to Ming Pao.

The purchases involved total consideration of about HK$173 million, the company said in an announcement.

data.title: Swiss National Bank Says Federal Council Proposal Targets Risks, Mainly Affecting UBS Group
data.description:
The Swiss National Bank said a proposal by the Federal Council provided targeted responses to the risks in question and would mainly affect UBS Group.

According to Jin10, the central bank said the Federal Council’s proposal was designed to address those risks in a focused way, with the primary impact falling on UBS Group.

data.title: STOCKS | India Textile Stocks Stand Out on Trade Deals
data.description:
Shares of Indian textile makers supplying T-shirts, bed linen and towels to global retailers including Walmart Inc. have been among this year’s biggest stock-market winners, according to Bloomberg.

Some investors are betting the rally has further to run, helped by trade deals.

data.title: Vietnam Central Bank Pledges to Support Growth Despite Rising Price Pressures
data.description: Vietnam’s central bank said it will try to balance support for economic growth with efforts to contain price pressures that are expected to intensify through the end of 2026, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Polymarket Odds Rise to 64% That GPT-5.6 Will Open to the Public on July 7
data.description:
Polymarket’s probability that “GPT-5.6 will open to the public on July 7” rose to 64%, up 26% over the past 24 hours. According to Odaily, the market’s rules define GPT-5.6 as a model explicitly named “GPT-5.6” or a model officially recognized as a direct successor to GPT-5.5, including versions such as GPT-5.7 or GPT-5.8.

The rules state that task-specific models such as GPT-Codex and Transcribe, cost-optimized versions such as Nano and Mini, and the o-series reasoning models are included in the market, while GPT-6 or other next-generation flagship models are excluded.

To qualify, the model must be made available to the public, including through a public beta or an open waitlist. Closed testing or private-only access does not meet the requirement. Settlement will primarily rely on OpenAI’s official announcements and publicly available information on its website, with verification based on mainstream media reports.

Odaily reported that on June 27, OpenAI introduced its next-generation GPT-5.6 series models, which are currently in a limited preview for a small number of partners.
